analysis.solution: Continue analyzing ping/port results. Use hostname target if DNS validation is needed.
suggestedChecks: If needed, rerun with a hostname to validate DNS path.

analysis.cause: No SIP service expected on this target.
analysis.solution: Only investigate SIP exposure if this host is meant to run VoIP signaling.
suggestedChecks: Confirm whether this host should provide SIP service before opening 5060.

analysis.issue: ICMP likely filtered on public IP
analysis.explanation: Ping failed and all tested ports are closed, which is common when public-facing firewalls drop ICMP and unsolicited inbound traffic.
analysis.context: This does not automatically mean outage. Many providers and edge firewalls intentionally block ping on WAN addresses.
analysis.cause: ICMP filtering and closed inbound policy on the public edge.
analysis.solution: Validate reachability using allowed management channels (VPN, Winbox allowlist, HTTPS) and confirm firewall policy intent.
suggestedChecks: Verify whether ICMP is intentionally blocked in input chain
Check if inbound policy is default-drop for non-whitelisted sources

primaryFinding.probableCause: Ping blocked and inbound ports closed from probe source; likely WAN edge filtering policy.
primaryFinding.recommendedAction: Treat as potentially expected hardening. Validate with allowed management path and ACL review.
mikrotikChecks: Check input chain policy in /ip firewall filter print
Validate support source IP allowlist for management
primaryFinding.routerOsCommand: /ip firewall filter print; /ip firewall address-list print
